pattern: Splitting the stream into a max-heap of the lower half and a min-heap of the upper half keeps the median at the heap tops, so each insert is logarithmic and each query is constant.
hints: You never need the whole sorted order — only the one or two values sitting in the middle.
Keep the smaller half and the larger half in two separate balanced collections so the boundary between them is always reachable in O(1).
Use a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half; after each insert rebalance so their sizes differ by at most one, then read the median from one top or the average of both.

edge_inputs.description: the statement's own sequence: median of one, then average of two, then middle of three
a single element is its own median
two equal values average to a whole number, returned as an int not a float
two values one apart give a .5 median
negatives and out-of-order inserts still find the true middle

stress.description: long interleaved insert/query stream over a small integer range so many medians are whole or half
stress.note: small value range keeps medians exact whole or half values; queries only after at least one insert

complexity.time: O(log n) per addNum, O(1) per findMedian
